name: Sunday Black-Bean Soup (New York Times)
recipeInstructions:
To soak beans quickly, cover them with a generous amount of water. Place the pot covered and bring the water to a boil for 2 minutes. Remove the cover and allow the beans to sit in the liquid for one hour. Drain.
In hot oil (1-2 tablespoons), sauté carrots, onions, celery, and garlic until the onions are golden. Add cumin and jalepenos.
When ready to cook, add ham hocks to the beans along with the sautéed vegetables and chicken broth, adding enough water to cover. Cook for 1½ to 2 hours, or until tender.
Drain the bean mixture and reserve the cooking liquid. Puree the beans and vegetables. Add sherry and lime juice, and enough of the reserved liquid to make a thick soup. This can be served with chopped coriander, sour cream, or lemon slice.
